properties.Mission:
The Oʻahu Invasive Species Committee (OISC) is a voluntary partnership of state, federal, and private agencies united to protect Oʻahu from the most harmful invasive plants and animals that threaten our environment, economy, and human health.

OISC works to prevent the establishment of new alien pests, controls incipient pests on public and private land, and educates the community about the threats of invasive species and what they can do to help.
properties.Metrics: Acres Surveyed; Mature Plants Removed; Immature Plants Removed; Work Hours; Volunteer Hours; Audience number and type of activity (event, school visit, community presentations, etc.); Social Media Engagement; Presentation Evaluations
